steps.title: Identify the revision pattern
steps.content: The key signal is the revision pattern itself: “Sometimes it was he and the second mate…but it was generally he and one other man. If not he and another man, then it was he by himself.” This shows Harris is constantly changing the details of his story about the rough crossing.

steps.title: Recognize the main idea
steps.content:
C correctly interprets the pattern: Harris is bragging about his own wellness by constantly revising the details about who else survived the rough crossing.

The other options either miss the revision pattern (A), mistake it for genuine memory loss (B), or address an unrelated topic (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
